records.ety: [OF. rondel a roundelay, F. rondel, rondeau, a dim. fr. rond; for sense 2, cf. F. rondelle a round, a round shield. See Round, a., and cf. Rondel, Rondelay.]

quotes: Come, now a roundel and a fairy song.

defs: Anything having a round form; a round figure; a circle.
A small circular shield, sometimes not more than a foot in diameter, used by soldiers in the fourteenth and fifteenth centuries.
A circular spot; a sharge in the form of a small circle.
A bastion of a circular form.
quotes: The Spaniards, casting themselves into roundels, . . . made a flying march to Calais.
